description: Configuration for config and runtime database connections; and distributed session cache settings in container deployments.

dsc.description: Configuration for the distributed session cache runtime containers.

ssl_keystore.description: SSL database to use to verify connections. Only valid if ssl == true.

user.description: Username to authenticate to database as.

db_name.description: Name of the database instance to use.

DistributedSessionCache.description: Distributed Session Cache configuration for container deployments.

client_grace.description: The length of time (in seconds) that a client (aka WebSEAL) has to reconnect before sessions owned by that client are discarded.

connection_idle_timeout.description: The maximum length of time that a connection from a client can remain idle before it is closed by the server. A value of 0 indicates that connections will not be reused.

max_session_lifetime.description: The maximum lifetime (in seconds) of any session stored by the DSC.

service_port.description: The port number on which the DSC will listen for requests.

replication_port.description: The port number on which the DSC will listen for replication requests.

ssl_ciphers.description: The comma separated list of permitted SSL algorithms for TLS connections to the DSC.

tls_12_cipher_specs.description: The comma separated list of permitted TLS 1.2 cipher specs for established TLS connections to the DSC.

tls_13_cipher_specs.description: The comma separated list of permitted TLS 1.3 cipher specs for established TLS connections to the DSC.

worker_threads.description: The number of worker threads allocated to processing requests.

servers.description: The external connection data for each instance of the DSC. This corresponds to the IP address and ports to which clients will connect. Up to 4 servers may be defined (primary, secondary, tertiary and quaternary). The role of the server will be determined by the order of elements within the servers array.

DSCServer.description: A Distributed Session Cache server configuration.

ip.description: The name/IP address over which clients can connect to the DSC.

service_port.description: The port which can be used by clients to connect to the DSC for session requests.

replication_port.description: The port which can be used by the DSC to replicate data to a replication DSC.
